summary: Joel Spolsky announces Fog Creek Software's annual summer open house event in New York City. The post invites software developers to visit their offices on July 19, 2007 to meet the team, interns, and management trainees. He uses the invitation as an opportunity to subtly showcase Fog Creek's developer-friendly work environment, mentioning private offices, large monitors, and premium chairs.
key_points: Fog Creek held annual summer open house events as a tradition to engage with the local developer community
The company maintained a developer-friendly office environment with private offices, 30-inch LCDs, and Aeron chairs — unusual enough to be worth showing off
Joel encourages attendees to bring their bosses, implicitly arguing that investing in developer workspace is both feasible and worthwhile
money_quotes: Bring your boss to prove how private offices, 30″ LCDs, and Aeron chairs are possible.
key_insight: Investing in premium developer workspaces is not only possible but worth showing off as a recruiting and retention advantage.
